import urllib.request
import urllib.error
from urllib.parse import urlparse
import re
import ssl
import certifi
import http.client


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    # Validate URL scheme
    parsed_url = urlparse(page_url)
    if parsed_url.scheme not in ('http', 'https'):
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Invalid URL scheme. Only HTTP and HTTPS are allowed.'
        }

    try:
        # Set up a request with a User-Agent header and a timeout of 10 seconds
        headers = {
            'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/58.0.3029.110 Safari/537.3'
        }
        request = urllib.request.Request(page_url, headers=headers)

        # Set up SSL context
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())

        # Fetch the content of the specified URL with a timeout
        with urllib.request.urlopen(request, context=context, timeout=10) as response:
            # Check Content-Type
            content_type = response.getheader('Content-Type', '')
            if content_type and 'text/html' not in content_type.lower():
                return {
                    'title': 'Content unavailable',
                    'description': 'Non-HTML content'
                }

            # Get the encoding from the Content-Type header
            encoding = None
            charset_match = re.search(r'charset=(\S+)', content_type)
            if charset_match:
                encoding = charset_match.group(1).strip('\'"')

            # Read the response bytes (first 64 KB)
            bytes_content = response.read(65536)

        # If no encoding was specified, try UTF-8 by default
        if encoding is None:
            encoding = 'utf-8'

        # Decode the content
        try:
            content = bytes_content.decode(encoding)
        except UnicodeDecodeError:
            # Fallback to UTF-8
            try:
                content = bytes_content.decode('utf-8')
            except UnicodeDecodeError:
                # Fallback to ISO-8859-1 (Latin-1) with replacement characters
                content = bytes_content.decode('iso-8859-1', errors='replace')

        # Extract title
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract meta description: two-step approach
        # Step 1: Find the meta tag with name="description"
        meta_tag_pattern = r'<meta\s[^>]*name\s*=\s*["\']description["\'][^>]*>'
        meta_tag_match = re.search(meta_tag_pattern, content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        description = "No description available"
        if meta_tag_match:
            meta_tag = meta_tag_match.group(0)
            # Step 2: Extract the content attribute from the meta tag string
            content_attr_pattern = r'content\s*=\s*["\'](.*?)["\']'
            content_attr_match = re.search(content_attr_pattern, meta_tag, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
            if content_attr_match:
                description = content_attr_match.group(1).strip()

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except (ValueError, urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError, UnicodeDecodeError, http.client.HTTPException) as e:
        # If the page cannot be fetched or parsed, return default values
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }

def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    try:
        # Fetch the content of the specified URL
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        with urllib.request.urlopen(page_url, context=context) as response:
            data = response.read()
            # Get charset from Content-Type header
            content_type = response.headers.get('Content-Type')
            charset = None
            if content_type:
                # Extract charset
                parts = content_type.split(';')
                for part in parts:
                    part = part.strip()
                    if part.startswith('charset='):
                        charset = part.split('=')[1].strip()
                        break

            # Decode content using detected charset or fallback to UTF-8/ISO-8859-1
            if charset:
                try:
                    content = data.decode(charset, errors='replace')
                except LookupError:  # invalid encoding
                    content = data.decode('utf-8', errors='replace')
            else:
                # Try UTF-8 first, then fallback to ISO-8859-1
                try:
                    content = data.decode('utf-8')
                except UnicodeDecodeError:
                    content = data.decode('ISO-8859-1', errors='replace')

        # Extract title
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract meta description using two patterns to handle attribute order
        # Pattern 1: name then content
        desc_match = re.search(
            r'<meta[^>]*\bname\s*=\s*["\']description["\'][^>]*\bcontent\s*=\s*["\'](.*?)["\']',
            content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL
        )
        if not desc_match:
            # Pattern 2: content then name
            desc_match = re.search(
                r'<meta[^>]*\bcontent\s*=\s*["\'](.*?)["\'][^>]*\bname\s*=\s*["\']description["\']',
                content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL
            )
        description = desc_match.group(1).strip() if desc_match else "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except (ValueError, urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError):
        # If the page cannot be fetched or parsed, return default values
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }

        }

    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}

    try:
        # Set headers to avoid being blocked and to accept compressed content
        headers = {
            'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/58.0.3029.110 Safari/537.3',
            'Accept-Encoding': 'gzip, deflate'
        }
        req = urllib.request.Request(page_url, headers=headers)

        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        # Set timeout to 10 seconds
        with urllib.request.urlopen(req, context=context, timeout=10) as response:
            # Read the response data
            data = response.read()

            # Check Content-Encoding
            content_encoding = response.headers.get('Content-Encoding', '').lower()
            if content_encoding == 'gzip':
                try:
                    data = gzip.decompress(data)
                except:
                    # Decompression failed, use the original data
                    pass
            elif content_encoding == 'deflate':
                try:
                    data = zlib.decompress(data)
                except:
                    pass

            # Determine charset from Content-Type header
            content_type = response.headers.get('Content-Type', '').lower()
            charset = 'utf-8'
            if 'charset=' in content_type:
                charset = content_type.split('charset=')[-1].split(';')[0].strip()

            # Decode the data using the charset, with fallbacks
            try:
                content = data.decode(charset)
            except (LookupError, UnicodeDecodeError):
                try:
                    content = data.decode('utf-8')
                except UnicodeDecodeError:
                    # If all else fails, use latin-1 and replace errors
                    content = data.decode('latin-1', errors='replace')

        # Extract title
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        desc_match = re.search(r'<meta[^>]*name=["\']description["\'][^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\']',
                              content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        description = desc_match.group(1).strip() if desc_match else "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except Exception:
        # If any error occurs, return default values
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
from urllib.parse import urlparse
import re
import ssl
import certifi
import ipaddress
import socket


def is_url_allowed(url: str) -> bool:
    """
    Check if the URL is allowed by our security policy.

    Args:
        url: The URL to check.

    Returns:
        True if allowed, False otherwise.
    """
    parsed = urlparse(url)
    # Check scheme
    if parsed.scheme not in ['http', 'https']:
        return False

    host = parsed.hostname
    if host is None:
        return False

    # Check if the host is an IP address
    try:
        # Try to parse as an IP address
        ip = ipaddress.ip_address(host)
        if ip.is_private or ip.is_loopback or ip.is_link_local:
            return False
        return True
    except ValueError:
        # It's a domain, resolve it
        try:
            # Resolve the domain to a list of IP addresses
            # We use getaddrinfo to handle both IPv4 and IPv6
            addrs = socket.getaddrinfo(host, None)
            for addr in addrs:
                # The addr structure: (family, type, proto, canonname, sockaddr)
                # For IPv4, sockaddr is (ip_address, port)
                # For IPv6, sockaddr is (ip_address, port, flow_info, scope_id)
                ip_str = addr[4][0]
                ip = ipaddress.ip_address(ip_str)
                if ip.is_private or ip.is_loopback or ip.is_link_local:
                    return False
        except:
            # If there is any error in resolution, we block
            return False

    return True


class SafeRedirectHandler(urllib.request.HTTPRedirectHandler):
    """
    A custom redirect handler that checks each redirect URL for security.
    """
    def redirect_request(self, req, fp, code, msg, headers, newurl):
        if not is_url_allowed(newurl):
            raise urllib.error.URLError(f"Redirect to URL not allowed: {newurl}")
        return super().redirect_request(req, fp, code, msg, headers, newurl)


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    try:
        # First, check if the URL is allowed
        if not is_url_allowed(page_url):
            raise ValueError("URL not allowed")

        # Create a custom opener with our redirect handler and HTTP/HTTPS handlers
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        opener = urllib.request.build_opener(
            SafeRedirectHandler(),
            urllib.request.HTTPHandler(),
            urllib.request.HTTPSHandler(context=context)
        )
        # Use the opener to open the request
        with opener.open(page_url) as response:
            content = response.read().decode('utf-8')

        # Extract title
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract meta description
        desc_match = re.search(r'<meta[^>]*name=["\']description["\'][^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\']',
                              content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        description = desc_match.group(1).strip() if desc_match else "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except (ValueError, urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError, UnicodeDecodeError):
        # Return default values if the page cannot be fetched or parsed
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from typing import Dict, Any
import re
import ssl
import certifi
import socket
import ipaddress

def is_safe_host(hostname: str) -> bool:
    """
    Determines if a hostname is safe to access by resolving its IP addresses and checking if they are globally routable.

    Args:
        hostname: The hostname to check.

    Returns:
        True if all resolved IP addresses are globally routable, False otherwise.
    """
    if not hostname:
        return False

    # Convert internationalized domain name to ASCII
    try:
        hostname = hostname.encode('idna').decode('ascii')
    except (UnicodeEncodeError, UnicodeDecodeError):
        pass

    try:
        # Resolve hostname to IP addresses
        addrinfo = socket.getaddrinfo(hostname, None)
    except socket.gaierror:
        return False

    for res in addrinfo:
        ip_str = res[4][0]
        try:
            ip = ipaddress.ip_address(ip_str)
        except ValueError:
            continue

        if not ip.is_global:
            return False

    return True

class SafeRedirectHandler(urllib.request.HTTPRedirectHandler):
    """
    Custom redirect handler that validates each redirect URL for safety.
    """
    def redirect_request(self, req, fp, code, msg, headers, newurl):
        # Parse the new URL
        parsed = urlparse(newurl)
        # Block non-HTTP/HTTPS schemes
        if parsed.scheme not in ['http', 'https']:
            return None
        # Block unsafe hosts
        if not is_safe_host(parsed.hostname):
            return None
        return super().redirect_request(req, fp, code, msg, headers, newurl)

def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    try:
        # Validate URL scheme
        parsed = urlparse(page_url)
        if parsed.scheme not in ['http', 'https']:
            return {
                'title': 'Content unavailable',
                'description': 'Invalid URL scheme'
            }

        # Validate host safety
        if not is_safe_host(parsed.hostname):
            return {
                'title': 'Content unavailable',
                'description': 'Access to the requested resource is blocked'
            }

        # Create SSL context
        context = ssl.create_default_context()

        # Build custom opener with security handlers
        handlers = [
            urllib.request.ProxyHandler({}),
            urllib.request.HTTPHandler(),
            urllib.request.HTTPSHandler(context=context),
            urllib.request.HTTPErrorProcessor(),
            SafeRedirectHandler()
        ]
        opener = urllib.request.build_opener(*handlers)

        # Fetch URL content
        with opener.open(page_url) as response:
            content = response.read().decode('utf-8')

        # Extract title
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract meta description
        desc_match = re.search(
            r'<meta[^>]*name=["\']description["\'][^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\']',
            content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL
        )
        description = desc_match.group(1).strip() if desc_match else "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except (urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError, UnicodeDecodeError, ValueError, socket.error):
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from typing import Dict, Any
import re
import ssl
import certifi


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    # Validate URL scheme
    parsed_url = urlparse(page_url)
    if parsed_url.scheme not in ('http', 'https'):
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Invalid URL scheme. Only HTTP/HTTPS supported.'
        }

    try:
        # Configure request with headers and timeout
        headers = {'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0 (compatible; social-media-scraper/1.0)'}
        req = urllib.request.Request(page_url, headers=headers)
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        
        # Fetch content with timeout
        with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10, context=context) as response:
            # Detect encoding from headers or default to UTF-8
            content_type = response.headers.get('Content-Type', '')
            charset = 'utf-8'
            if 'charset=' in content_type:
                charset = content_type.split('charset=')[-1].strip()
            
            # Decode content with error handling
            content = response.read().decode(charset, errors='replace')

        # Extract title with regex
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Improved description regex (handles attribute order and self-closing tags)
        desc_pattern = re.compile(
            r'<meta\s+(?:[^>]*?\bname\s*=\s*["\']description["\'][^>]*?|'
            r'[^>]*?\bcontent\s*=\s*["\'](.*?)["\'][^>]*?)+>',
            re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL
        )
        desc_match = desc_pattern.search(content)
        if desc_match:
            # Extract content attribute value directly
            content_attr = re.search(r'content=["\'](.*?)["\']', desc_match.group(0), re.IGNORECASE)
            description = content_attr.group(1).strip() if content_attr else "No description available"
        else:
            description = "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except Exception as e:
        # Catch all potential errors during fetch/parse
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }

def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:

            'description': description

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from typing import Dict, Any
import re
import ssl
import certifi


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    try:
        # Create SSL context for secure connection
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        # Fetch the content of the specified URL
        with urllib.request.urlopen(page_url, context=context) as response:
            data = response.read()
            content_type = response.headers.get('Content-Type', '')
            charset = 'utf-8'
            # Extract charset from content-type header if available
            if content_type:
                match = re.search(r'charset=([\w-]+)', content_type)
                if match:
                    charset = match.group(1).lower()

            # Decode using the detected charset, if that fails try UTF-8
            try:
                content = data.decode(charset)
            except (LookupError, UnicodeDecodeError):
                # If the encoding is not found or decoding fails, try UTF-8
                content = data.decode('utf-8')

        # Extract title
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract meta description: two-step approach
        # Step 1: Find the meta tag with name="description" or name='description'
        desc_tag_pattern = r'<meta[^>]*name=("|\')description\1[^>]*>'
        desc_tag_match = re.search(desc_tag_pattern, content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        description = "No description available"
        if desc_tag_match:
            desc_tag = desc_tag_match.group(0)
            # Step 2: Extract the content attribute from the found tag
            content_attr_pattern = r'content=(["\'])(.*?)\1'
            content_attr_match = re.search(content_attr_pattern, desc_tag, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
            if content_attr_match:
                description = content_attr_match.group(2).strip()

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except (urllib.error.URLError, UnicodeDecodeError, ValueError, ssl.SSLError):
        # If the page cannot be fetched or parsed, return default values
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }

import re
from urllib.request import Request, urlopen
from urllib.error import URLError, HTTPError
import ssl
import certifi

def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches metadata (title and description) from a webpage.
    
    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public webpage.

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing 'title' and 'description' keys with extracted values.
        If extraction fails, returns empty strings for values.
    """
    # Set up SSL context using certifi
    context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
    
    # Configure headers to mimic a browser
    headers = {
        'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/58.0.3029.110 Safari/537.3'
    }
    
    try:
        # Create request with headers and open with SSL context
        req = Request(page_url, headers=headers)
        with urlopen(req, timeout=10, context=context) as response:
            # Read and decode content with proper encoding handling
            content_type = response.headers.get('Content-Type', '')
            charset = 'utf-8'
            
            # Extract charset from content-type header if available
            if 'charset=' in content_type:
                charset_match = re.search(r'charset=([\w-]+)', content_type)
                if charset_match:
                    charset = charset_match.group(1)
            
            html = response.read().decode(charset, errors='ignore')
            
            # Extract title using regex
            title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', html, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
            title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else ''
            
            # Extract description from meta tags
            description = ''
            meta_match = re.search(
                r'<meta\s+(?:name|property)=["\'](?:description|og:description)["\']\s+content=["\'](.*?)["\']',
                html,
                re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL
            )
            if meta_match:
                description = meta_match.group(1).strip()
            
            return {
                'title': title,
                'description': description
            }
    
    except (URLError, HTTPError, ValueError, OSError):
        # Handle any network or processing errors gracefully
        return {'title': '', 'description': ''}

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from typing import Dict, Any
import re
import ssl
import certifi


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    try:
        # Fetch the content of the specified URL
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        with urllib.request.urlopen(page_url, context=context) as response:
            data = response.read()
            charset = None
            content_type = response.headers.get('Content-Type', '')
            if content_type:
                # Extract charset from Content-Type header
                match = re.search(r'charset=(\S+)', content_type)
                if match:
                    charset = match.group(1).strip().lower()
                    # Clean charset string
                    charset = charset.strip('"').strip("'").split(';')[0]

            if charset:
                try:
                    content = data.decode(charset)
                except (LookupError, UnicodeDecodeError):
                    # Fallback if detected encoding fails
                    charset = None
            
            if not charset:
                # Try common encodings
                for encoding in ['utf-8', 'iso-8859-1', 'windows-1252']:
                    try:
                        content = data.decode(encoding)
                        break
                    except UnicodeDecodeError:
                        continue
                else:
                    # Final fallback with error ignoring
                    content = data.decode('utf-8', errors='ignore')

        # Extract title
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract meta description
        desc_match = re.search(r'<meta[^>]*name=["\']description["\'][^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\']',
                              content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        description = desc_match.group(1).strip() if desc_match else "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except (urllib.error.URLError, ValueError, OSError):
        # Catch all fetch/parse related errors
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }

import urllib.error
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from typing import Dict, Any

import ipaddress

def is_blocked_ip(ip_str: str) -> bool:
    """
    Check if an IP address string is in a blocked category.

    Args:
        ip_str: IP address string to check.

    Returns:
        True if IP is blocked (private, loopback, link-local, multicast, or reserved), False otherwise.
    """
    try:
        ip = ipaddress.ip_address(ip_str)
        return (
            ip.is_private or
            ip.is_loopback or
            ip.is_link_local or
            ip.is_multicast or
            ip.is_reserved
        )
    except ValueError:
        return False

def validate_url(url: str) -> None:
    """
    Validate URL against security constraints.

    Args:
        url: URL to validate.

    Raises:
        ValueError: If URL fails any security check.
    """
    parsed = urlparse(url)
    
    # Check URL scheme
    if parsed.scheme not in ['http', 'https']:
        raise ValueError("Invalid URL scheme")
    
    # Check for valid hostname
    if not parsed.hostname:
        raise ValueError("Invalid hostname")
    
    # Check hostname for IP address blocks
    if is_blocked_ip(parsed.hostname):
        raise ValueError("Blocked IP address")
    
    # Resolve hostname to IP(s) and check each
    try:
        addrinfo = socket.getaddrinfo(parsed.hostname, None)
        for res in addrinfo:
            ip = res[4][0]  # Extract IP from address info
            if is_blocked_ip(ip):
                raise ValueError("Blocked IP address resolved")
    except socket.gaierror:
        raise ValueError("DNS resolution failed")

def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

        # Extract title
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract meta description

        description = desc_match.group(1).strip() if desc_match else "No description available"

import urllib.error
import socket
from typing import Dict, Any
import re
import ssl
import certifi

def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    # Set a realistic User-Agent and timeout
    user_agent = 'Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/58.0.3029.110 Safari/537.3'
    timeout = 10  # seconds

    try:
        # Create request with User-Agent header
        req = urllib.request.Request(page_url, headers={'User-Agent': user_agent})
        
        # Create SSL context with certificate verification
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        
        # Fetch the content with timeout
        with urllib.request.urlopen(req, context=context, timeout=timeout) as response:
            data = response.read()
            
            # Try different encodings
            content = None
            for encoding in ['utf-8', 'iso-8859-1', 'windows-1252']:
                try:
                    content = data.decode(encoding)
                    break
                except UnicodeDecodeError:
                    continue
                    
            if content is None:
                content = data.decode('utf-8', errors='replace')

        # Extract title with improved regex
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract description with more robust regex
        desc_pattern = r'<meta\s+(?=[^>]*?\bname\s*=\s*[\'"]?\bdescription\b[\'"]?)(?=[^>]*?\bcontent\s*=\s*[\'"](.*?)[\'"])[^>]*>'
        desc_match = re.search(desc_pattern, content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        description = desc_match.group(1).strip() if desc_match else "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except (urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError, socket.timeout) as e:
        # If the page cannot be fetched or parsed, return default values
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from typing import Dict, Any
import re
import ssl
import certifi


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    try:
        # Set up SSL context and request headers
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        headers = {'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/58.0.3029.110 Safari/537.3'}
        req = urllib.request.Request(page_url, headers=headers)
        
        # Fetch the content with timeout
        with urllib.request.urlopen(req, context=context, timeout=10) as response:
            raw_data = response.read()
            
            # Detect encoding from headers or default to UTF-8
            encoding = response.headers.get_content_charset()
            if not encoding:
                encoding = 'utf-8'
            
            # Try decoding with detected encoding, fallback to ISO-8859-1 if needed
            try:
                content = raw_data.decode(encoding)
            except (UnicodeDecodeError, LookupError):
                content = raw_data.decode('iso-8859-1', errors='replace')

        # Extract title with improved regex
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract meta description with more robust regex
        desc_pattern = r'<meta\s+[^>]*?name\s*=\s*["\']description["\'][^>]*?content\s*=\s*["\'](.*?)["\']'
        desc_match = re.search(desc_pattern, content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        description = desc_match.group(1).strip() if desc_match else "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except (urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError, ssl.SSLError, ValueError, TimeoutError) as e:
        # Handle various network and parsing errors
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from typing import Dict, Any
import re
import ssl
import certifi


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    # Validate the URL scheme to prevent non-HTTP/HTTPS requests (such as file://)
    parsed_url = urlparse(page_url)
    if parsed_url.scheme not in ['http', 'https']:
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }

    try:
        # Fetch the content of the specified URL
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        with urllib.request.urlopen(page_url, context=context) as response:
            content = response.read().decode('utf-8')

        # Extract title
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract meta description
        desc_match = re.search(r'<meta[^>]*name=["\']description["\'][^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\']',
                              content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        description = desc_match.group(1).strip() if desc_match else "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except (urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError, UnicodeDecodeError):
        # Return default values if the page cannot be fetched or parsed
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }
